chief_complaint: A 6-year case series of patients with chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension (CTEPH) undergoing lung transplantation (LTx) at the Alfred Hospital.
history: The patients had CTEPH, a form of pulmonary vascular disease amenable to multimodal therapy. Pulmonary endarterectomy (PEA) is the cornerstone of CTEPH treatment for operable patients, but a significant proportion present with inoperable disease or residual postoperative pulmonary hypertension. LTx remains a lifesaving therapy for patients with end-stage right heart failure due to CTEPH.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: The most frequent finding on explant histology was arterial muscular hypertrophy due to thrombotic arteriopathy. Pre-transplant hemodynamic features and long-term outcomes post-LTx were discussed.
